subject: [PATCH v4 0/8] mm: switch THP shrinker to list_lru

2026-05-21.patch_summary: This patch series (v4) aims to switch the THP shrinker from its current open-coded deferred split queue to using the list_lru data structure. The existing implementation has issues, including being non-NUMA-aware and complicated in its interactions with other code. Switching to list_lru fixes these problems, simplifies the code, and streamlines the interaction between the THP shrinker and LRU state. The series includes several cleanup patches (1-4) that prepare for this change, followed by extensions to the list_lru API (5-6) and finally the switch itself in patch 8.

reviews.author: Usama Arif
reviews.summary:
Reviewer Usama Arif noted that deferred_split_folio() takes the lock via list_lru_lock_irqsave() with the original folio's memcg, but __list_lru_add() sets the shrinker bit on the dying/reparented child memcg instead of the parent that owns the locked sublist where the folio was queued.

Reviewer Usama Arif noted that the current implementation of list_lru will report success even when allocation fails, and suggested setting the result to SCAN_ALLOC_HUGE_PAGE_FAIL in such cases.
reviews.raw_body:
Can the shrinker bit end up on the wrong memcg here?

deferred_split_folio() takes the lock via list_lru_lock_irqsave() with
the original folio_memcg() of the folio. If that memcg is dying and
already reparented, lock_list_lru_of_memcg() walks up parent_mem_cgroup()
until it finds a live sublist and locks it (lru), but the memcg local
variable still points at the dying child.

__list_lru_add() then calls set_shrinker_bit(memcg, ...) with the
original (dying / reparented) memcg, not the parent that actually owns
the locked sublist where the folio was queued.

---

Over here, you will end up reporting success on allocation failure.

Maybe set result to SCAN_ALLOC_HUGE_PAGE_FAIL?

reviews.author: Johannes Weiner (author)
reviews.summary:
The author acknowledged that there is an existing issue with list_lru_add() calling lock_list_lru_of_memcg(), which might do a hierarchy walk, and agreed to fix this in the list_lru code first before rebasing their patches on top.

Author acknowledged the need to address an issue pointed out by multiple reviewers, including Usama and Sashiko, and promised a follow-up fix.
reviews.raw_body:
I think you're right. Good catch.

This looks like an existing list_lru issue, actually. Even before,
list_lru_add() would call lock_list_lru_of_memcg() which might do the
hierarchy walk. But then set_shrinker_bit() on the passed in memcg.

I'll fix this in list_lru first. It's a likely backport
candidate. Then rebase my patches on top.

---

Good point. Sashiko pointed that out as well. I have a follow-up fix.

Thanks for taking a look.

2026-05-26.patch_summary: This patch series (v4) aims to switch the THP shrinker from its current open-coded deferred split queue to using the list_lru data structure. The existing implementation has issues, including being non-NUMA-aware and complicated in its interactions with other code. Switching to list_lru fixes these problems, simplifies the code, and streamlines the interaction between the THP shrinker and LRU state. The series includes several cleanup patches (1-4) that prepare for this change, followed by extensions to the list_lru API (5-6) and finally the switch itself in patch 8.
